title: Tech Process and Product Review
series: Tech Process and Product Review Series
summary: Michael Donovan discussed leveraging AI for deep research and visualization, sharing how Google Sites can host interactive AI-generated content, and introduced his marketing automation platform to consolidate marketing experience, offering it as a resource for collaboration. Claude Hayn and Audrey Hayn shared their focus on small to medium enterprises, particularly HVAC, and explained their approach to selling solutions rather than technology, emphasizing the intelligence of their development team, who are given high-level vision and autonomy. Claude Hayn also outlined plans to integrate their current website into a new communication suite, incorporating Michael Donovan's marketing platform capabilities and Fonoster, while Michael Donovan demonstrated an MVP of a conversational CRM for car dealerships and used market analysis to discuss exclusivity.

decisions.description: Focus on small to medium enterprises, specifically HVAC, for VCON-integrated solutions.
Strategy to sell solutions and product capabilities rather than just technology.
Developers will be given high-level vision and autonomy for implementation.
Integrate current website into a new communication suite, moving off WordPress.
Incorporate Michael Donovan's marketing platform capabilities into the new communication suite.
Integrate Fonoster, an open-source Twilio alternative, into the communication suite.
Michael Donovan will deploy the marketing automation platform.
Audrey Hayn and Claude Hayn will explore the marketing automation platform.
Michael Donovan will send documentation link for the environment design.
Claude Hayn and Audrey Hayn will receive login credentials for the platform next week.

actionItems.task: Explore the marketing automation platform as a closed beta resource.
Explore the marketing automation platform as a closed beta resource.

tensions: Difficulty educating HVAC providers about VCON without extensive background explanation.
Competition with siloed platforms offering transcriptions and deep RAM.
VCON alone lacks high value and needs 'meat substance' for practical solutions.
Struggle to implement AI for process automation in Audrey Hayn's side job due to lack of understanding.
Frustration with external SEO vendors using outdated methods (2008 approach).

Michael Donovan's agency's attribution model from 2015/2016 for car dealerships.
Claude Hayn's patent partner Carl Ford's lack of ad performance insight leading to 'ad jumps'.
Michael Donovan's 20 years of marketing experience consolidated into his platform.

contributions.description: Demonstrated leveraging AI (Google Gemini) for deep research and infographic visualization.
Proposed using Google Sites to host interactive AI-generated content and knowledge bases.
Introduced his marketing automation platform, consolidating 20 years of experience with AI agents.
Shared focus on small to medium enterprises, specifically HVAC, after market analysis.
Emphasized selling solutions and product capabilities ('the steak') over technology ('the sizzle').
Explained giving developers high-level vision and autonomy due to their intelligence.
Stated VCON needs 'meat substance' and practical solutions to provide high value beyond transcription.
Suggested using Cursor with Claude Desktop and GitHub's SpecKit for spec-driven development.
Recommended Shad CN, an open-source Tailwind CSS library, for UI development.
Outlined plans to integrate their website, Michael Donovan's platform, and Fonoster into a new communication suite.
Demonstrated an MVP of a conversational CRM for car dealerships, aiming to be a disruptor.
Suggested offering market exclusivity for services like HVAC to create scarcity and demand.
Expressed interest in using Michael Donovan's methods to implement AI for process automation in her side job.
